name: GaugeFlow Gate A10 species-aware site-representation audit v1
objective: Determine whether the residual A7-A9 type failures are arbitrary CIF-index permutations or true species-decorated crystal mismatches, without training, retuning, or changing a historical report.

structure_matcher.definition: Match species-decorated periodic structures after allowed lattice and atom permutations; a failed match is a real chemical-sublattice mismatch, not merely a CIF row-order difference.

code_audit_scope: EndpointIdConditionEncoder emits zero edge response fields.
ResponseMessageLayer scalar messages currently receive node features, condition features, and response/direction invariants, but no periodic edge distance or vector-state invariant.
With zero response and a fully masked type state, the type scalar head consequently lacks a direct periodic metric signal for site discrimination.
decision_rule: Do not add more discrete path losses or sampler schedules. A future architecture proposal must make type scalar messages geometrically informative through periodic metric/vector invariants and state how i.i.d. node noise resolves unlabeled-site symmetries without using target atom order.
